site.title: The Black Ledger: Philippine Political Corruption Archive
site.subtitle: A future-dictionary style scroll archive of documented Philippine corruption cases, watchdog findings, court records, and regional patterns.
site.disclaimer: This website distinguishes convictions, court findings, official charges, audit findings, and allegations. A person or office mentioned as charged or investigated is not treated as convicted unless a court record says so.

stats.label: CPI 2025 score
stats.note: Transparency International reported the Philippines at 32 out of 100 in CPI 2025, where 0 means highly corrupt and 100 means very clean.

stats.label: Anti-graft court
stats.note: Special court with jurisdiction over graft and corruption cases involving public officers.

stats.label: PDAF audit sample
stats.note: A public audit case study summarized COA findings on PDAF releases reviewed for 2007 to 2009.
stats.source: International Budget Partnership / COA audit case study

stats.label: Recovered wealth mandate
stats.note: The Presidential Commission on Good Government is mandated to assist in recovering ill-gotten wealth accumulated by former President Marcos and related parties.

timeline.title: Martial-law era wealth recovery issue
timeline.body: The Marcos-era corruption story is not just a campaign argument. The PCGG exists specifically because the post-1986 government created a mechanism to recover alleged ill-gotten wealth accumulated during the Marcos administration and by close associates.

timeline.title: Joseph Estrada plunder conviction
timeline.body: The Supreme Court e-library record notes that on September 12, 2007, the Sandiganbayan found former President Joseph Estrada guilty beyond reasonable doubt of plunder. He was later pardoned.

timeline.title: PDAF / pork barrel scandal moves to court
timeline.body: The PDAF scandal centered on the alleged diversion of public development funds to ghost or deficient projects through NGOs. Ombudsman actions led to charges against several national figures.
timeline.status: Official charges and prosecutions; mixed outcomes by accused person

timeline.title: Jinggoy Estrada bribery conviction in PDAF case
timeline.body: PNA reported that the Sandiganbayan found Jinggoy Estrada guilty of one count of direct bribery and two counts of indirect bribery while acquitting him of plunder in the PDAF case.
timeline.status: Bribery conviction reported by state news agency

timeline.title: Infrastructure and flood-control scrutiny
timeline.body: Recent reporting and financial rules connected anti-corruption scrutiny to infrastructure and flood-control spending. The site labels these as ongoing investigations unless a court conviction exists.

cases.name: Ferdinand Marcos Sr. / Marcos-era asset recovery
cases.status: Asset-recovery mandate and litigation history; not every listed asset claim has succeeded in court
cases.summary: The Marcos case is the long shadow in Philippine anti-corruption history: not one clean courtroom ending, but decades of recovery actions, forfeiture cases, partial wins, dismissals, and political memory wars.
cases.keywords: ferdinand marcos sr. / marcos-era asset recovery ilocos region / national ill-gotten wealth recovery asset-recovery mandate and litigation history; not every listed asset claim has succeeded in court

cases.name: Joseph Estrada
cases.status: Convicted by Sandiganbayan in 2007; later pardoned
cases.summary: The case remains one of the clearest examples of a former Philippine president being convicted for plunder by the anti-graft court.
cases.keywords: joseph estrada ncr / national plunder convicted by sandiganbayan in 2007; later pardoned

cases.name: PDAF / Pork Barrel Scam
cases.status: Multiple cases, convictions, acquittals, and pending histories depending on accused person
cases.summary: This scandal turned congressional discretionary spending into a national keyword for fake NGOs, ghost projects, and development money that allegedly never reached communities.
cases.keywords: pdaf / pork barrel scam national, multiple regions affected public fund diversion / ghost projects multiple cases, convictions, acquittals, and pending histories depending on accused person

cases.name: Jinggoy Estrada PDAF case
cases.status: Convicted of bribery counts in 2024; acquitted of plunder in that case
cases.summary: A useful example of why labels matter: the case did not end as a plunder conviction, but did produce bribery convictions according to state reporting.
cases.keywords: jinggoy estrada pdaf case ncr / national bribery / pdaf convicted of bribery counts in 2024; acquitted of plunder in that case

cases.name: Flood-control and infrastructure cases
cases.status: Ongoing investigations and recent charges reported in 2025–2026
cases.summary: Flood-control corruption stories hit harder because the public sees the receipt in real life: floods, broken roads, half-built protection, and citizens paying twice, once in taxes and again in damage.
cases.keywords: flood-control and infrastructure cases multiple regions infrastructure procurement / alleged kickbacks ongoing investigations and recent charges reported in 2025–2026

regions.name: NCR
regions.story: Metro Manila is where many national cases surface because the Senate, major agencies, Sandiganbayan, Ombudsman, and Supreme Court machinery are concentrated here. Corruption here often looks like national-budget control, procurement influence, and legal spectacle.
regions.watch: PDAF, national procurement, presidential and senatorial cases.

regions.name: Ilocos Region
regions.story: The region is tied to the Marcos political legacy. The corruption history is mostly national in scale but regionally rooted through political identity, family power, and the unresolved public argument over recovered and unrecovered wealth.
regions.watch: Ill-gotten wealth narratives, dynasty politics, public memory.

regions.name: Cagayan Valley
regions.story: Like many large agricultural and infrastructure regions, corruption risk often appears through roads, irrigation, disaster funds, and procurement. The lesson is not that every official is corrupt, but that thin oversight makes budget lines easy to hide behind.
regions.watch: Farm support, road works, disaster funds.

regions.name: Central Luzon
regions.story: Central Luzon regularly appears in public concern over flood-control and infrastructure because geography makes floods expensive. Where water-control budgets grow, so does the need for audit trails.

regions.name: CALABARZON
regions.story: Fast growth creates opportunities for clean investment and dirty shortcuts. Zoning, permits, procurement, housing, and local business influence become pressure points.

regions.name: MIMAROPA
regions.theme: Resources and distance from central oversight
regions.story: Island governance makes monitoring harder. Ports, mining, tourism permits, and logistics spending can become corruption risks when public visibility is low.
regions.watch: Mining permits, port projects, tourism funds.

regions.name: Bicol Region
regions.story: Frequent typhoons mean repeated emergency spending. That is exactly when good governance matters most, because urgency can become an excuse to skip scrutiny.

regions.name: Western Visayas
regions.story: Ports, tourism, and city-level procurement create both development and temptation. The clean version builds mobility; the corrupt version builds invoices.
regions.watch: Port upgrades, tourism infrastructure, city contracts.

regions.name: Central Visayas
regions.story: Cebu-centered development brings complex procurement networks. Corruption risk rises when growth moves faster than transparency.

regions.name: Eastern Visayas
regions.story: After major disasters, reconstruction money becomes a moral test. The region's history shows why relief, shelter, and road spending need traceable public records.

regions.name: Zamboanga Peninsula
regions.story: Security and logistics spending can be difficult for ordinary citizens to audit. That makes public documentation and procurement transparency more important.
regions.watch: Port works, security-related procurement, local bidding.

regions.name: Northern Mindanao
regions.story: As a logistics and growth corridor, public works become central. Every bridge and road project needs a paper trail citizens can inspect.

regions.name: Davao Region
regions.theme: PDAF-linked case history and local power
regions.story: Davao del Sur appears in PDAF-related case history through former local officials and pork-barrel misuse proceedings. The broader regional issue is the concentration of political power and public spending oversight.

regions.name: SOCCSKSARGEN
regions.story: Agricultural support funds can be life-changing when clean and invisible when abused. Seeds, equipment, livelihood projects, and farm inputs need end-to-end tracking.

regions.name: Caraga
regions.story: Resource-rich areas face corruption risk around permits, royalties, enforcement, and road access. Oversight should follow both money and minerals.

regions.name: BARMM
regions.story: Autonomy makes governance reform possible, but also raises stakes for transparent institutions. Budget tracking matters because public trust is still being built.
regions.watch: Block grants, local institutions, procurement systems.

regions.name: Cordillera Administrative Region
regions.story: Mountain roads, mining questions, and local autonomy make the region sensitive to opaque contracts. Transparency protects both communities and land.
regions.watch: Road works, mining issues, indigenous community consultation.

glossary.definition: A high-value public-office corruption offense under Philippine law. This site labels a case as plunder only when the source record says so.
Priority Development Assistance Fund, often called pork barrel, tied to major public fund diversion cases.
The Philippines anti-graft court handling many corruption cases involving public officers.
Commission on Audit, the constitutional body that audits public funds and public agencies.
Presidential Commission on Good Government, created after 1986 to pursue recovery of alleged ill-gotten wealth.
A case can be alleged, investigated, charged, acquitted, convicted, or pending. Those labels are not interchangeable, despite what comment sections pretend.
